text: So this is dissection of this tumor, which is very vascular.
And soon you will see as we try to take it down off of the chest wall, you'll start to visualize the subclavian artery.
Again, you can see the complexity of all these blood vessels that are coming out from the adventitia of the subclavian artery and going directly into the tumor.
Here you can see the pulsating structure in the background, and that's the subclavian artery.
You can see the feeding vessels clipped right adjacent to the tumor, and here the tumor comes out without any significantly large insertion and so forth.
And similarly, this lady was home in three days.
